outcome_contract.why: Staff using the rooms grid have no at-a-glance view of how many rooms are occupied tonight. They must count occupied cells manually when deciding whether to push discounts, adjust minimum stays, or block availability.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: A compact occupancy count strip appears on the rooms-grid page showing the number of occupied rooms vs total rooms for the current date, derived from existing booking data without additional network requests.

engineering_coverage.applicable: New leaf component `OccupancyStrip`, DS primitives, token-based colors
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Minimal: one new component using established DS patterns

engineering_coverage.applicable: Strip shows count, zero-occupied, full-house, today-outside-window states; no new loading spinner needed
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Adds a second loading spinner for strip data independently
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Option A: no new loading state; strip defers to RoomsGrid loading gate

engineering_coverage.applicable: N/A — internal staff tool, no new data pathway
Props-driven component: count logic in pure utility function; strip renders props directly — straightforward RTL test
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Requires mocking new Firebase hook; more test surface
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Option A is far simpler to test

engineering_coverage.applicable: No schema or type changes; uses existing `GridReservationRow[]` and `TBookingPeriod`
`useMemo` for count derivation; O(rooms × bookings) — bounded; no new I/O

engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Git revert is the rollback mechanism

stage_payload.decision_summary: How should the occupancy count for tonight be computed and displayed on the rooms-grid page? The fact-find confirmed that all required data is already available in `useGridData`. The core decision is: derive the count client-side from the existing hook, or introduce a new Firebase query. A secondary decision is the component shape: props-driven leaf vs hook-calling component.
goals: Display occupied room count vs total rooms for the current date
Zero additional network requests beyond what already loads
Purely additive change — no existing behaviour modified
Clean test seam: computation logic is separately testable
non_goals: Per-day occupancy grid/heatmap (deferred to future iteration)
New API endpoints or server routes
constraints: DS primitives only (`Cluster`, `Inline`, `Stack` from `@acme/design-system/primitives`). No raw flex or inline styles.
Typecheck + lint must pass locally before commit.
Tests written for CI; no local jest execution.
assumptions: A room is "occupied tonight" if it has at least one `GridReservationRow` with a `TBookingPeriod` where `period.start <= today < period.end` AND `period.status` is not in `{"free", "disabled", "16"}`

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Reception data loads slowly from Firebase; any new query degrades perceived performance
Props-driven components are trivially testable; hook-calling components require more mocking surface
P3 feature; should not introduce architectural complexity
The count should correctly reflect tonight's occupancy given the booking data already loaded

options_considered.Option: A — Derive from existing hook data (client-side, zero new queries)
options_considered.Description: Compute occupancy count in `RoomsGrid` from already-loaded `getReservationDataForRoom` data; pass `occupiedCount` + `totalRooms` as props to a new `OccupancyStrip` leaf component
options_considered.Upside: Zero additional fetches; clean prop interface; easy to unit-test; additive only
options_considered.Downside: Count is only as current as the already-loaded data (same currency as the grid itself)
options_considered.Key risks: Today might fall outside the selected date window — handle with a null/absent state in the strip

options_considered.Option: B — New Firebase query for tonight's occupancy
options_considered.Description: Add a new `useOccupancyData` hook that queries Firebase independently for tonight's bookings
options_considered.Downside: Adds a new subscription, new loading state, new error path, new mocking surface in tests; all data is already available from Option A
options_considered.Key risks: Increased complexity, extra cost, and slower load time with no accuracy benefit

chosen_approach.Recommendation: Option A — derive occupancy count client-side from `getReservationDataForRoom` data already loaded by `useGridData`.
chosen_approach.Why this wins: All data is already available. Zero new network requests. Props-driven component with a pure computation utility is trivially testable. The count is exactly as current as the rest of the grid (same data source), which is correct and expected. Option B adds complexity with no accuracy or performance benefit.
chosen_approach.What it depends on: `useGridData(startDate, endDate)` being called in `RoomsGrid` (it already is); `getReservationDataForRoom` returning `GridReservationRow[]` per room (confirmed); `knownRooms` from `useRoomConfigs` (already available in `RoomsGrid`).

rejected_approaches: Option B (new Firebase query) — All required data is already loaded by `useGridData`. A second query adds latency, a new error/loading state, additional test mocking surface, and no accuracy benefit over the data already in memory.

validation_implications: `pnpm --filter @apps/reception lint` — must pass (ds/enforce-layout-primitives, no raw flex)
sequencing_constraints: Single task or two tasks (component + wiring) — implementation is simple enough for one task

process_topology.note: None: no material process topology change. The strip is a read-only additive UI element on an existing page. No workflow, approval path, or runbook is affected.
